data.culture_summary: Egypt, officially the Arab Republic of Egypt, is a country spanning the northeast corner of Africa and southwest corner of Asia via the Sinai Peninsula. It is bordered by the Mediterranean Sea to the north, Palestine and Israel to the northeast, the Red Sea to the east, Sudan and the Sahara to the south, and Libya to the west. The Gulf of Aqaba in

expat_origins.typical_roles: Construction labor, domestic work, retail, hospitality
Executive roles, consulting, oil & gas, NGOs

pay_scales.notes: Includes bonuses and allowances, often tax-advantaged for expats
Engineers, doctors, senior managers; expats often receive housing allowances
Mid-level roles in finance, IT, healthcare; expats may receive relocation support
Technicians, electricians, mechanics; expats often in specialized industries
Construction, retail, drivers; expats mostly in informal sectors
Agriculture, cleaning, unskilled labor; expats often South/Southeast Asian

workforce_detail.labour_law_notes: Minimum wage: EGP 3,500/month (public sector); EGP 2,700/month (private sector as of 2024). Maximum 48-hour workweek, 21 days paid leave after 1 year. Social insurance mandatory for Egyptians; expats may opt out with proof of foreign coverage. Termination requires 2 months' notice or pay. Strikes heavily regulated; unions exist but are government-aligned.

hofstede.pd_meaning: Hierarchical structures in retail and labor markets require clear authority figures for consumer trust and decision-making.
hofstede.idv_meaning: Collectivist values drive group-based purchasing decisions, such as family or community bulk buying and shared loyalty programs.
hofstede.mas_meaning: Moderate masculinity suggests a balance between competitive pricing and quality, with emphasis on practical, durable goods over status symbols.
hofstede.uai_meaning: High uncertainty avoidance leads to preference for trusted brands, detailed product information, and risk-averse spending behaviors.
hofstede.lto_meaning: Short-term orientation prioritizes immediate needs and traditional values, limiting long-term investment in innovative or unproven products.
hofstede.ivr_meaning: Low indulgence reflects restrained spending on non-essentials, with savings and practicality prioritized over luxury or hedonic consumption.

monthly_spending.marketing_activation: Launch post-holiday clearance campaigns with bundled discounts on electronics and apparel.
monthly_spending.notes: High spending on gifts and personal indulgences post-New Year; Coptic Christmas drives food and gift purchases.

monthly_spending.marketing_activation: Promote Valentine's Day gift sets with personalized messaging options for jewelry and chocolates.
monthly_spending.notes: Romantic spending peaks; mid-month sales for Valentine's Day drive category-specific uplift.

monthly_spending.marketing_activation: Partner with florists and restaurants for Mother's Day bundles, including digital vouchers for dining.
monthly_spending.notes: Mother's Day and Sham El-Nessim drive food, gifts, and family outings; traditional foods like salted fish and onions see high demand.

monthly_spending.marketing_activation: Introduce Ramadan pre-sale discounts on bulk food items and home essentials.
monthly_spending.notes: Easter drives food and toy sales; Ramadan preparations begin, with early stockpiling of groceries.

monthly_spending.marketing_activation: Launch Ramadan-themed digital campaigns with iftar meal kits and family gift bundles.
monthly_spending.notes: Highest spending month; food, clothing, and gifts dominate. Night markets and online sales surge during Ramadan.

key_events: Eid al-Fitr (early June, if Ramadan ends late May)
monthly_spending.weather_effect: extremely hot, travel and indoor spending increase
monthly_spending.marketing_activation: Promote Eid al-Fitr travel packages and summer fashion lines with early-bird discounts.
monthly_spending.notes: Eid al-Fitr drives gift-giving and travel; summer sales begin, with discounts on electronics and apparel.

monthly_spending.weather_effect: peak heat, travel and indoor spending
monthly_spending.marketing_activation: Collaborate with travel agencies for last-minute summer getaway deals and back-to-school bundles.
monthly_spending.notes: Tourism peaks; early back-to-school shopping begins for uniforms and supplies.

monthly_spending.marketing_activation: Offer back-to-school bundles with discounts on notebooks, uniforms, and tech gadgets for students.
monthly_spending.notes: Highest back-to-school spending; summer sales conclude with clearance discounts.

monthly_spending.marketing_activation: Promote academic year essentials with student discounts and bulk purchase deals on stationery.
monthly_spending.notes: Final back-to-school purchases; Coptic New Year sees modest spending on food and small gifts.

monthly_spending.marketing_activation: Launch Eid al-Adha campaigns with meat delivery services and family gift bundles.
monthly_spending.notes: Eid al-Adha drives livestock purchases and gift-giving; wedding season begins, boosting apparel and jewelry sales.

monthly_spending.marketing_activation: Introduce Black Friday digital campaigns with flash sales and limited-time discounts on electronics and fashion.
monthly_spending.notes: Wedding season peaks; Black Friday drives electronics and apparel sales; Sufi Moulids increase local spending on food and crafts.

monthly_spending.marketing_activation: Promote holiday gift guides with curated product bundles and last-minute delivery options.
monthly_spending.notes: Highest spending month after Ramadan; Christmas and New Year drive gifts, food, and apparel sales.

celebration_events.note: Coptic Christians (10% of population) observe a strict fast before the holiday; retail sales spike in clothing, gifts, and food items like kahk and biscuits.
Public holiday with patriotic sales, discounts on electronics and home appliances, and increased media advertising.
Limited commercial impact; some promotions on domestic travel and local retail in Sinai region.
Workers receive bonuses; increased spending on clothing, electronics, and automotive services; B2B gifting for employees.
Major shopping season; peak sales in clothing, electronics, home goods, and food (sweets, meat); travel bookings surge; malls extend hours.
High demand for livestock, meat, and sacrificial services; retail sales spike in clothing, electronics, and home goods; travel for Hajj and family visits.
Moderate spending on sweets, gifts, and home decorations; some promotions in retail and food sectors.
Increased spending on sweets (e.g., ma'amoul), gifts, and charity donations; retail promotions in clothing and electronics.
Coptic Christians observe a 55-day fast; post-Easter sales spike in clothing, gifts, and food items like feseekh and colored eggs.
Ancient Egyptian spring festival; families picnic outdoors; high demand for traditional foods (feseekh, salted fish, onions, lettuce) and travel.
Limited commercial impact; some patriotic promotions and media coverage.
Minimal commercial impact; some local promotions in Suez.
Patriotic sales, discounts on electronics and home appliances, and increased media advertising.
Peak sales in clothing, electronics, and luxury goods; New Year's Eve parties and travel bookings surge.
Egypt's version of Black Friday; major online and offline discounts, especially on Noon and Amazon Egypt; peak e-commerce sales.
Nightly iftar and suhoor meals drive food sales; retail sales spike in clothing, electronics, and home goods; TV viewership peaks; malls extend hours.
High demand for gifts, flowers, and dining out; promotions in retail and hospitality sectors.
Moderate spending on gifts and dining out; promotions in retail and hospitality.
High demand for gifts, flowers, and romantic dining; conservative backlash may limit public celebrations.
Peak sales in school supplies, uniforms, electronics, and tutoring services; private schools drive higher spending.
High demand for graduation gowns, gifts, photography, and celebratory dining; private universities drive luxury spending.
Increased demand for study materials, tutoring, and café study sessions; parents spend on private lessons.
Peak demand for tutoring, study materials, and café study sessions; parents invest heavily in private lessons.
Peak travel to coastal resorts (North Coast, Ain Sokhna, Hurghada); high sales in swimwear, sunscreen, and summer fashion; malls and cinemas see increased footfall.
Increased sales in winter clothing, heaters, and hot beverages; domestic travel to Luxor and Aswan peaks.
Pink-themed promotions in retail; healthcare providers offer discounted screenings; media campaigns drive awareness.
Gym memberships and fitness apparel sales spike; health food and supplement sales increase.
Businesses purchase gifts for clients and employees; high demand for luxury items, electronics, and corporate dining.
Government tenders and contracts drive B2B spending in construction, IT, and services; retail sales to government agencies increase.
Peak demand for wedding dresses, jewelry, venues, catering, and photography; high spending on luxury items and gold.
High demand for engagement rings, jewelry, and celebratory dining; gold sales spike.
Patriotic sales and media campaigns; limited commercial impact compared to other national holidays.
Increased spending on cinema tickets, dining, and hospitality; media coverage drives tourism.
Local impact in Alexandria; moderate spending on entertainment and hospitality.
High demand for fashion items; media coverage drives retail sales and brand awareness.
Shia Muslim observance; limited commercial impact; some spending on sweets and gifts.
Public celebrations with sweets, parades, and entertainment; increased spending on food and gifts.
Largest book fair in the Arab world; high demand for books, stationery, and educational materials.
TV viewership peaks; advertisers spend heavily on Ramadan-themed ads; new series and shows drive media revenue.
Fintech companies offer discounts and cashback; drives digital payment adoption and e-commerce sales.
Major online sales event; discounts on electronics, fashion, and home goods; drives e-commerce growth.
Noon.com's annual sales event; deep discounts on electronics, fashion, and home goods; boosts online shopping.
Jumia's Black Friday sales; major discounts on electronics, fashion, and home goods; drives e-commerce traffic.
Summer sales on Souq.com (now Amazon Egypt); discounts on electronics, fashion, and travel.
Post-Eid sales clear inventory; deep discounts on clothing, electronics, and home goods; malls and online platforms see high traffic.
Post-Eid sales clear inventory; discounts on home goods, electronics, and fashion; livestock and meat sales decline post-Eid.
Egyptian e-commerce platforms offer mid-year sales; discounts on electronics, fashion, and home goods.
Banks and financial institutions promote savings products; limited consumer impact.
Largest ICT event in Egypt; B2B networking, tech exhibitions, and government tenders drive business spending.
Construction and infrastructure event; B2B networking and government contracts drive spending.
Energy sector event; B2B networking, exhibitions, and government contracts drive business spending.
Limited commercial impact; government and NGO participation drives minimal B2B spending.
Promotions on Egyptian cotton products; limited consumer impact but boosts textile industry.
Promotions on chocolates and sweets; limited but targeted consumer spending.
Promotions on ice cream and desserts; seasonal spike in sales during summer.
